problems.problem: Wire Feed Motor Runs but Wire Does Not Feed Properly

causes_and_solutions.cause: Insufficient wire feed pressure
causes_and_solutions.solution: Increase wire feed pressure properly. Follow step 27 on page 17.

causes_and_solutions.cause: Incorrect wire feed roller size
causes_and_solutions.solution: Flip roll to correct size. Follow the Feed Roller instructions on page 12.

causes_and_solutions.cause: Damaged MIG Gun, cable, or liner assembly
causes_and_solutions.solution: Have a qualified technician inspect these parts and replace as necessary.

causes_and_solutions.cause: Feed Tensioner is too tight
causes_and_solutions.solution: Loosen Feed Tensioner so it applies only enough pressure to prevent continued spinning after the Gun Trigger is released.

problems.problem: Wire Creates a Bird's Nest During Operation

causes_and_solutions.cause: Excess wire feed pressure
causes_and_solutions.solution: Adjust wire feed pressure properly. Follow step 27 on page 17.

causes_and_solutions.cause: Incorrect Contact Tip size
causes_and_solutions.solution: Replace with the proper tip for wire used.

causes_and_solutions.cause: MIG Gun Cable Connector not fully inserted into Wire Feed mechanism
causes_and_solutions.solution: Insert Gun Cable Connector properly. Follow steps 13 and 14 on page 13.

causes_and_solutions.cause: Damaged liner
causes_and_solutions.solution: Have a qualified technician inspect and repair or replace as necessary.

causes_and_solutions.cause: Gun cable is severely bent and Wire Feed mechanism cannot feed wire
causes_and_solutions.solution: Straighten Gun cable.

causes_and_solutions.cause: Gun liner is clogged or worn
causes_and_solutions.solution: Check gun liner for obstruction. Replace if necessary.

causes_and_solutions.cause: Gun liner is too small for welding wire being used
causes_and_solutions.solution: Check that gun liner is correct size for wire.

causes_and_solutions.cause: Wire is tangled on the spool
causes_and_solutions.solution: Check wire for cross winding or tangled spool.

causes_and_solutions.cause: Wire is not making contact with Feed Rollers
causes_and_solutions.solution: Check Feed Rollers and ensure correct groove for wire diameter is being used.

causes_and_solutions.cause: Feed Roller is not making enough contact with wire or is crushing flux-cored wire
causes_and_solutions.solution: Check Feed Tensioner and ensure it is set properly.

causes_and_solutions.cause: Wire not feeding properly
causes_and_solutions.solution: See Wire Feed Motor troubleshooting section above.

causes_and_solutions.cause: Incorrect Contact Tip or liner size or excessive wear
causes_and_solutions.solution: Replace with the proper tip or liner size for wire used.

causes_and_solutions.cause: Incorrect wire feed speed
causes_and_solutions.solution: Adjust wire feed speed to achieve a more stable arc.

causes_and_solutions.cause: Loose MIG Gun cable or ground cable
causes_and_solutions.solution: Check to ensure that all connections are tight.

causes_and_solutions.cause: Damaged MIG Gun or loose connection within Gun
causes_and_solutions.solution: Have a qualified technician inspect and repair/replace as necessary.

causes_and_solutions.cause: Incorrect polarity for process being run
causes_and_solutions.solution: Ensure polarity is correct for operation: DCEP for MIG welding and DCEN for Flux-Cored self-shielded welding.

causes_and_solutions.cause: Gas coverage may be insufficient or too high
causes_and_solutions.solution: Ensure gas flow rate is set according to Settings Chart. Make sure MIG Gun Cable Connector is fully inserted into Wire Feed mechanism with no O-Rings exposed.

causes_and_solutions.cause: Poor connection with workpiece
causes_and_solutions.solution: Check the ground clamp connection to the workpiece and machine. Ensure the MIG Gun is properly secured.

causes_and_solutions.cause: Incorrect line voltage
causes_and_solutions.solution: Check the line voltage and, if insufficient, have a licensed electrician remedy the situation.

causes_and_solutions.cause: Improper gauge or length of cord
causes_and_solutions.solution: Do not use an extension cord on this Welder. Use only one of the supplied power cords for this Welder or an identical replacement cord.

causes_and_solutions.cause: Not enough current
causes_and_solutions.solution: Switch current to proper setting for metal thickness.

problems.problem: Welder Does Not Function When Switched On

causes_and_solutions.cause: Tripped thermal protection device
causes_and_solutions.solution: If a warning screen appears in the LCD Display window, Welder may have overheated and shut down. Stop and wait with the Power Switch ON for the Welder to cool. The Welder automatically returns to service after cooling off. Reduce duration or frequency of welding periods to help reduce wear on the Welder. Refer to Duty Cycle section on page 19.

causes_and_solutions.cause: Circuit supplies insufficient input voltage or amperage
causes_and_solutions.solution: Verify that the circuit is designed to supply the required input voltage and amperage as detailed on the Specifications table. If a warning screen appears in the LCD Display window, check the input voltage to ensure it is within specified range.

causes_and_solutions.cause: Faulty or improperly connected Trigger
causes_and_solutions.solution: Ensure the gun connection is properly seated on machine. Qualified technician must check and secure/replace Trigger.

causes_and_solutions.cause: Machine is in low- or over-voltage protection
causes_and_solutions.solution: Check input voltage and ensure it falls within the specified range. If input voltage is correct, press Reset Button on back of machine.

causes_and_solutions.cause: Machine is in the incorrect mode
causes_and_solutions.solution: Ensure the correct process has been selected.

problems.problem: LCD Display Does Not Light When Welder is Switched On

causes_and_solutions.cause: Unit is not connected to outlet properly
causes_and_solutions.solution: Verify the voltage at the outlet and the connection to the outlet.

causes_and_solutions.cause: Outlet is unpowered
causes_and_solutions.solution: Check circuit breaker/GFCI devices; if any are tripped, determine and remedy cause before resetting.

causes_and_solutions.cause: Plug does not have correct rating
causes_and_solutions.solution: Make sure installed plug is correct rating. See Specifications on page 7.

causes_and_solutions.cause: Circuit breaker has tripped due to high input amperage
causes_and_solutions.solution: Press Reset Button on back of machine to reset circuit breaker.

causes_and_solutions.cause: Input Power Cord is not seated properly
causes_and_solutions.solution: Ensure the twist lock input Power Cord is fully secured.

problems.problem: Wire Feeds, but Arc Does Not Ignite

causes_and_solutions.cause: Improper ground connection
causes_and_solutions.solution: Make certain that the workpiece is contacted properly by the Ground Clamp and that the workpiece is properly cleaned near the ground clamp and the welding location.

causes_and_solutions.cause: Improperly sized Contact Tip
causes_and_solutions.solution: Verify that Contact Tip is the proper size for welding wire. If needed, replace Contact Tip with proper size and type.

causes_and_solutions.cause: Excessively worn Contact Tip
causes_and_solutions.solution: Check that the hole in the tip is not deformed or enlarged. If needed, replace Contact Tip with proper size and type.

causes_and_solutions.cause: Dirty Contact Tip
causes_and_solutions.solution: Properly clean Contact Tip.

causes_and_solutions.cause: Shielding gas bottle is empty
causes_and_solutions.solution: Check gas bottle and replenish as necessary.

causes_and_solutions.cause: Not enough or too much shielding gas
causes_and_solutions.solution: Check gas regulator to ensure proper flow.

causes_and_solutions.cause: Dirty workpiece
causes_and_solutions.solution: Clean workpiece down to bare metal.

causes_and_solutions.cause: Gun is being used too far away from workpiece
causes_and_solutions.solution: Check CTWD (contact tip to work distance) for the proper procedure.

causes_and_solutions.cause: Polarity is incorrect for the application
causes_and_solutions.solution: Check the polarity and ensure it is DCEP for MIG and DCEN for Flux-Cored.

causes_and_solutions.cause: Dirty welding wire is introducing contamination into the weld
causes_and_solutions.solution: Make certain that welding wire is clean and free of rust and residues.

problems.problem: Welder Does Not Function When Switched On

causes_and_solutions.cause: Tripped thermal protection device
causes_and_solutions.solution: Reduce duration or frequency of welding periods to help reduce wear on the welder. Refer to Duty Cycle (Duration of Use) on page 29.

causes_and_solutions.cause: Faulty or improperly connected Trigger
causes_and_solutions.solution: Qualified technician must check and secure/replace Trigger.

causes_and_solutions.cause: Ground Clamp not attached to workpiece
causes_and_solutions.solution: Attach Ground Clamp to workpiece.

causes_and_solutions.cause: Shielding Gas not connected
causes_and_solutions.solution: Connect shielding gas to Welder.

problems.problem: LCD Display Does Not Light When Welder is Switched On

causes_and_solutions.cause: Unit is not connected to outlet properly
causes_and_solutions.solution: Verify the voltage at the outlet and the connection to the outlet.

causes_and_solutions.cause: Outlet is unpowered
causes_and_solutions.solution: Check circuit breaker/GFCI devices; if any are tripped, determine and remedy cause before resetting. Verify that the circuit is designed to supply the required input amperage as detailed in Specifications on page 7.

causes_and_solutions.cause: Incorrect line voltage
causes_and_solutions.solution: Check the line voltage and, if insufficient, have a licensed electrician remedy the situation.

causes_and_solutions.cause: Improper gauge or length of cord
causes_and_solutions.solution: Do not use an extension cord on this Welder. Use only one of the supplied power cords for this Welder or an identical replacement cord.

causes_and_solutions.cause: Loose electrode cable or ground cable
causes_and_solutions.solution: Check to ensure that all connections are tight.

causes_and_solutions.cause: Damaged electrode holder or loose connection within electrode holder
causes_and_solutions.solution: Have a qualified technician inspect and repair/replace as necessary.

causes_and_solutions.cause: Adjust current setting
causes_and_solutions.solution: Make sure setting matches recommended setting on chart.

causes_and_solutions.cause: Shielding gas getting low
causes_and_solutions.solution: Replace shielding gas cylinder.

wire_weld_examples.appearance: Voltage Too Low or Wire Feed Too Slow
wire_weld_examples.action: Increase Output Voltage or Increase Wire Feed Speed

wire_weld_examples.appearance: Voltage Too High or Wire Feed Too Fast
wire_weld_examples.action: Decrease Output Voltage or Decrease Wire Feed Speed

wire_weld_examples.appearance: CTWD Too Long or Wrong Polarity
wire_weld_examples.action: Check polarity and maintain less than 1/2 inch CTWD
